chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Cr. Bail App. No. S – 02 of 2024 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge For hearing of bail application 1. For orders on office objections at Flag-A 2. For hearing of bail application 26.01.2024 Mr. Maqsood Ahmed Leghari, Advocate for applicants along with applicants, who has filed a statement along with certain documents and case law. Mr. Iqbal Hussain Joyo, Advocate, who has filed power on behalf of complainant. Mr. Aftab Ahmed Shar, Additional Prosecutor General..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro, J. – Applicants and complainant are closely related and on a dispute over land matter. Applicants are stated to have caused injuries to complainant on 20.10.2022 at about 07:00 a.m. inside his house. One injury opined by Medico Legal Officer is 337-F(v) PPC, whereas the remaining injuries are other hurts. 2. Learned Counsel for applicants has pleaded for bail, not opposed by learned Additional Prosecutor General, but learned Counsel for complainant has opposed bail. However, applicants, who are cousins of complainant, have sought pardon from complainant, who is present in the Court and has agreed to negotiate with them for the purpose of compromise. 3. In any case, there is a delay of 15 days in registration of FIR. The injury sustained by complainant is punishable only for 05 years and does not fall under prohibitory clause of Section 497(i) CrPC. 4. Therefore, this application is allowed, and ad-interim pre-arrest bail already granted to applicants vide order dated 01.01.2024 is hereby confirmed on the same terms and conditions. 5. The observations, as above, are tentative in nature and not meant to affect merits of the case before the trial Court.

Therefore, this application is allowed, and ad-interim pre-arrest bail already granted to applicants vide order dated 01.01.2024 is hereby confirmed on the same terms and conditions. 5. The observations, as above, are tentative in nature and not meant to affect merits of the case before the trial Court. The bail application stands disposed of in the above terms. J U D G E Abdul Basit
